finding.contract_claim: Symbol `get_file_tools` is declared but appears unused — no references were found outside its own declaration.
finding.observed_behavior: No callers or importers of `get_file_tools` were found in the fixture project; consumer modules import the get_*_tool_definitions() variants instead, so this wrapper is never invoked even though the TOOL constant it returns is alive.

finding.triage_reasoning: Reality: The evidence sweep over 6 files found zero callers of `get_file_tools`. Consumer modules (e.g. `src/osoji/shadow.py`) import `get_file_tool_definitions()` (which returns `list[ToolDefinition]`), not `get_file_tools()` (which returns `list[dict]`). The typed `ToolDefinition` variant superseded the raw-dict variant; the raw-dict wrapper is never invoked. Significance: The dead wrapper adds noise and confusion about which API to use. Actionability: Remove `get_file_tools` (lines 311-313); the live `get_file_tool_definitions()` already serves all callers.
finding.suggested_fix: Remove the `get_file_tools()` function (lines 311-313). All callers use `get_file_tool_definitions()` instead.
